Title: The bacterial effector AvrRxo1 inhibits vitamin B6 biosynthesis to promote infection in rice

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes stomatal closure and immunity to Xoc.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis; stomatal aperture assay; bacterial growth assay
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: ABA-insensitive bzip23 mutants show compromised VB6/ABA-induced stomatal closure and increased susceptibility to Xoc.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as an ABA signaling component required for VB6-induced stomatal closure, but not the primary nutrient metabolism gene of the study.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: VB6 treatment; ABA treatment; Xoc infection
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for strengthening ABA-mediated stomatal defense, though direct nutrient-engineering value was not addressed.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. bZIP23 mediates ABA signaling required for VB6-induced stomatal closure. 2. The bzip23 mutant is more susceptible to Xoc. 3. It links VB6-triggered defense to ABA signaling.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes stomatal closure and immunity to Xoc.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: ABA-deficient osaba1 mutants are more susceptible to Xoc, supporting positive role of ABA in defense.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Serves as genetic evidence that ABA deficiency compromises immunity; not a central nutrient metabolism focus relative to OsPDX1/AvrRxo1.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Supports use of ABA-biosynthesis strengthening alleles for disease resistance.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. The osaba1 mutant is more susceptible to Xoc. 2. This supports a positive role of ABA biosynthesis in rice immunity. 3. It provides pathway-context evidence downstream of VB6.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Participates in de novo vitamin B6 biosynthesis to produce pyridoxal 5'-phosphate/vitamin B6 vitamers.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Cofactor for numerous enzymes; required for aldehyde oxidase activity and thus ABA biosynthesis; promotes stomatal immunity against Xoc.
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Cell periphery/border of epidermal cells (interaction signal with AvrRxo1 in BiFC)
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast two-hybrid; BiFC; coIP; in vitro degradation assay; overexpression; CRISPR-Cas9 knockout; RNAi; VB6 metabolite profiling; bacterial growth assay; stomatal aperture assay; ABA quantification; aldehyde oxidase activity assay
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes VB6 accumulation, increases ABA levels, promotes stomatal closure, and enhances resistance to Xoc; loss reduces VB6 and increases susceptibility.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control point not established; major de novo VB6 biosynthesis gene implicated
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct target of bacterial effector AvrRxo1, which degrades the protein in an ATP-dependent manner; overexpression increased VB6 by 25%-95%, knockout lowered VB6 and ABA.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for engineering increased VB6 nutrition and improved bacterial leaf streak resistance; overexpression could couple nutrient quality and immunity.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsPDX1.2 physically interacts with the Xoc effector AvrRxo1 and is degraded by it in an ATP-dependent manner. 2. OsPDX1.2 overexpression elevates VB6 and ABA, reduces stomatal aperture, and enhances resistance to Xoc. 3. OsPDX1.2 knockout lowers VB6 and ABA and increases susceptibility to Xoc.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Participates in de novo vitamin B6 biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports cofactor supply for metabolism and immunity-associated ABA biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast two-hybrid; plant interaction assay; overexpression; RNAi; VB6 metabolite profiling; bacterial growth assay
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes positively to VB6 biosynthesis and resistance to Xoc; overexpression enhances resistance, while co-silencing with other OsPDX1 genes lowers VB6 and resistance.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Interacts with AvrRxo1 and forms homo-/heterodimers with rice PDX1 proteins; functional evidence mostly from combined RNAi and overexpression supplemental data.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential gene-stacking partner with OsPDX1.2 to increase VB6 and disease resistance.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsPDX1.1 is a rice PDX1 family member involved in VB6 biosynthesis. 2. It interacts with AvrRxo1 and with other PDX1 proteins. 3. Constitutive expression enhances resistance to Xoc, whereas collective OsPDX1 silencing lowers VB6 and resistance.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Participates in de novo vitamin B6 biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports cofactor supply for metabolism and immunity-associated ABA biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast two-hybrid; plant interaction assay; overexpression; RNAi; VB6 metabolite profiling; bacterial growth assay
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes positively to VB6 biosynthesis and resistance to Xoc; overexpression enhances resistance, while co-silencing with other OsPDX1 genes lowers VB6 and resistance.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Interacts with AvrRxo1 and forms homo-/heterodimers with rice PDX1 proteins; functional evidence mostly from combined RNAi and overexpression supplemental data.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential gene-stacking partner with OsPDX1.2 to improve VB6 content and resistance.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsPDX1.3 is a rice PDX1 family member involved in VB6 biosynthesis. 2. It interacts with AvrRxo1 and with other PDX1 proteins. 3. Constitutive expression enhances resistance to Xoc, whereas collective OsPDX1 silencing lowers VB6 and resistance.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Activates molybdenum cofactor by converting desulfo-Moco into sulfurated Moco required for aldehyde oxidase activity in the final step of ABA biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Moco activation supporting the terminal ABA-forming step
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes stomatal closure and rice immunity against Xoc.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression; bacterial growth assay; aldehyde oxidase activity context from pathway model
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression enhances resistance to Xoc, consistent with increased ABA biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key supportive step for terminal ABA synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussed mechanistically as VB6-dependent cofactor-using step; overexpression data directly show positive effect on resistance.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for improving ABA-mediated stomatal defense and disease resistance, potentially in combination with OsPDX1 genes.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsABA3 overexpression increases resistance to Xoc. 2. ABA3 functions in Moco activation needed for aldehyde oxidase activity in ABA biosynthesis. 3. The study places VB6 upstream of ABA3-dependent ABA production.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Vitamin B6 biosynthesis via degradation of biosynthetic enzymes
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Promotes degradation of PDX1 proteins, thereby reducing VB6 biosynthesis and indirectly lowering ABA biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports ABA production and stomatal immunity; contributes to grain nutritional quality.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major virulence factor that directly suppresses host VB6 accumulation and consequently ABA-mediated stomatal immunity
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: OsPDX1.2; OsPDX1.1; OsPDX1.3; AtPDX1.1; AtPDX1.2; AtPDX1.3; SNZ1
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Acts on host PDX1 proteins that can form homo-/heterodimers
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Effector with ATP-binding motif and protease motif; also reported putative kinase/NAD kinase-related catalytic residue
Regulation_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Cell periphery/border of epidermal cells when interacting with OsPDX1.2
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene knockout mutant; complementation; virulence assay; stomatal aperture assay; Y2H; BiFC; coIP; in vitro protease assay; ATP dependence assay; metabolite profiling of VB6 and ABA
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhances Xoc virulence by reopening stomata, reducing host VB6 and ABA levels, and lowering nutritional VB6 content in infected rice grains.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Protease activity on OsPDX1.2 requires ATP; ATP-binding mutants K166N and T167A lose degradation activity, while D193A retains it, indicating putative kinase activity is dispensable for protease-mediated virulence.
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoter in complementation; DEX-inducible promoter in transgenic Arabidopsis, inducible
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Xoc infection; dexamethasone induction in Arabidopsis
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Target for disease-control strategies by disabling effector delivery/function or by engineering PDX1 variants resistant to AvrRxo1-mediated degradation.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AvrRxo1 is required for Xoc to reopen rice stomata and achieve full virulence after surface inoculation. 2. It directly interacts with and degrades rice OsPDX1 proteins in an ATP-dependent manner. 3. This lowers VB6 and ABA levels, suppresses stomatal immunity, and reduces VB6 nutritional quality of infected rice grains.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Acts as pathogen virulence factor; no host breeding deployment except resistance strategies.
